---
title: "exploratory_analysis"
author: "Fournier Raphaël"
format: html
editor: source
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This file calculates, visualises and summarises the exploratory analyses from  the individual differences face familiarity study.

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

pkgs <- c(
  "EMC2", "tidyverse", "tidybayes", "patchwork", "paletteer", "here",
  "corrplot", "ggdist", "RColorBrewer", "parallel", "future", "brms", "tidyr",
  "ggplot2", "dplyr", "ggpubr"
  )
vapply(
  pkgs, library, logical(1), character.only = TRUE, logical.return = TRUE
  )

theme_set(
  theme_bw() +
    theme(
      text  = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
      title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
)

## set options
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores(),
        future.fork.enable = TRUE,
        future.rng.onMisuse = "ignore") ## automatically set in RStudio

supportsMulticore()

detectCores()

plan(multicore)

## make things reproducible where possible 
set.seed(123)

safe_pal   <- paletteer_d("rcartocolor::Safe", 4)
mycolors <- c("novel" = safe_pal[1], "learned" = safe_pal[2])
```

# 1. read in the data

```{r}
#| label: read-wrangle

# read in posterior medians and cambridge scores
theta_pid_q <- read_csv(
  here("data", "theta_pid_q.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
cam_face_car <- read_csv(
  here("data", "cambridge_acc_scores_pid_res.csv"))


# drift rate data — same structure as pilot
drift_data <- theta_pid_q |>
  filter(str_detect(param, "v_S")) |>
  select(pid, param, value) |>
  mutate(
    stimulus = if_else(str_detect(param, "novel"), "novel", "learned"),
    accuracy = if_else(str_detect(param, "TRUE"), "true", "false"),
    condition = factor(
      paste0(stimulus, ".", accuracy),
      levels = c("learned.false", "novel.false", "learned.true", "novel.true")
    ),
    stimulus = factor(stimulus, levels = c("learned", "novel")),
    accuracy = factor(accuracy, levels = c("false", "true"))
  ) |>
  left_join(cam_face_car, by = "pid") |>
  select(pid, condition, stimulus, accuracy, face, car, value)

# threshold data
thresh_data <- theta_pid_q |>
  filter(str_detect(param, "B_")) |>
  select(pid, param, value) |>
  mutate(
    response = if_else(str_detect(param, "novel"), "novel", "learned"),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("learned", "novel"))
  ) |>
  left_join(cam_face_car, by = "pid") |>
  select(pid, response, face, car, value)

# check structure
head(drift_data)
head(thresh_data)
```

# 2. Calculate d' and Criterion from the accuracy data of the face recognition taks

```{r}
#read in the face recognition task
data_task <- read_csv(here("data", "data.csv"))

#create d' dataframes: separate data frames for learned and novel (we will put them back together later)
dprime_final <- d_prime %>%
  group_by(pid) %>%
  summarise(
    # Learned condition
    HR_learned = mean(resp_type_learned == "hit", na.rm = TRUE),
    FAR_learned = mean(resp_type_learned == "fa",  na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Novel condition
    HR_novel = mean(resp_type_novel == "hit", na.rm = TRUE),
    FAR_novel = mean(resp_type_novel == "fa",  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dprime_learned = qnorm(HR_learned) - qnorm(FAR_learned),
    dprime_novel   = qnorm(HR_novel)   - qnorm(FAR_novel)
  )
```

#Adding d'prime column in drift dataframe
```{r}
cor_data <- theta_pid_q |>
  select(pid, param, value) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = param, values_from = value) |>
  left_join(dprime_final, by = "pid") |>
  # derived contrasts
  mutate(
    # drift discrimination (true - false) per condition
    discrim_novel   = v_Snovel_lMTRUE   - v_Snovel_lMFALSE,
    discrim_learned = v_Slearned_lMTRUE - v_Slearned_lMFALSE,
    # cross-condition drift contrast (novel - learned discrimination)
    discrim_diff    = discrim_novel - discrim_learned,
    # threshold contrast
    thresh_diff     = B_lRnovel - B_lRlearned
  )
```


Correlations
```{r}
param_cols <- c(
  "drift_learned", "drift_novel"
)

# pivot to long with both predictors as a variable
cor_long <- cor_data |>
  select(pid, discrim_novel, discrim_learned, dprime_learned, dprime_novel)|>
  rename(drift_learned = "discrim_learned", drift_novel = "discrim_novel") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = all_of(param_cols),
    names_to  = "param",
    values_to = "estimate"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(dprime_learned, dprime_novel),
    names_to  = "predictor",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    predictor = if_else(str_detect(predictor, "learned"), "sensitivity learned", "sensitivity novel"),
    predictor = factor(predictor, levels = c("sensitivity learned", "sensitivity novel"))
  )

# compute r per param x predictor
cor_labels <- cor_long |>
  group_by(param, predictor) |>
  summarise(
    r = cor(estimate, score, use = "complete.obs"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    label = sprintf("r = %.2f", r),
    vjust = if_else(predictor == "sensitivity learned", 1.5, 3.0)
  )

# Extract the two panels' data
df_plot <- cor_long %>%
  # Keep only the matching condition pairs
  filter(
    (param == "drift_learned" & predictor == "sensitivity learned") |
    (param == "drift_novel"   & predictor == "sensitivity novel")
  ) %>%
  mutate(condition = case_when(
    param == "drift_learned" ~ "Learned",
    param == "drift_novel"   ~ "Novel"
  ))

# Plot
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = score, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top") + # requires ggpubr
  facet_wrap(~ condition, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    x = "Sensitivity (d')",
    y = "Drift rate",
    title = "Drift rate ~ Sensitivity by condition"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave(here("figures", "corr", "d_prime.jpeg"), 
       width = 10, height = 8)
```
